_comment_crosshost: Single host: leave the keys below as-is. Cross-host (one realm across machines, §7): set bind to 0.0.0.0 and profile.discovery to tailscale — then this whole file can be Dropbox-shared verbatim across machines, because advertiseHost auto-derives from `tailscale status` per machine. Set advertiseHost only to override the auto-derived address. Same token on every machine = same realm. (Windows firewall must allow inbound TCP on the control port over the Tailscale interface.)
_comment_authorizer: authorizer (§16) gates presence-confirmed actions — today: taking over your OWN offline (dormant) topic from another session. 'none' (default) denies such takeovers; 'hello' raises a real Windows Hello prompt via the tray (proven in experiments/hello-tpm-vault, needs live wiring); 'script' is for tests/headless. allowCrossUserTakeover: may a DIFFERENT user take over a dormant topic after its grace window? Default false (conservative); a claim can override per-topic via grace_minutes + allow_other_user on claim_topic.
_comment_vault: vault (§21) enables secret RECOVERY: the bridge seals each session's secret at registration (encrypt-to-the-user) so a session that loses it (a compact throws away the bearer secret) can recover via recover_secret + a presence check. 'none' (default) = no recovery (operator must clear stale state); 'tpm' = seal to the Windows TPM key + Windows Hello unseal (needs Tpm.exe, built by tray/windows/build-tpm.cmd); 'script' = reversible, for tests. Requires persistence on (the sealed secret lives in the persistence vault store).
_comment_persistence: Set profile.persistence to 'file' (or env AI_BRIDGE_PERSISTENCE=file) to turn persistence ON; 'none' (default) = unchanged ephemeral behaviour. BUILT (§12): durable messages (park — survive a restart, redelivered on re-register), responsibilities (claims durable by default), cross-project grants (§14), registrations (offline-by-name park, §19), subscriptions, and retained last-value-per-topic — all five stores. `dir` is the shared folder (Dropbox here, or an SMB/NFS path) and may differ from where this config lives; env AI_BRIDGE_PERSIST_DIR overrides it. Timers shown with defaults so they're visible/tunable; messageTtlDays can be overridden per message; claimHardExpiryDays is the lease GC for abandoned claims. mailboxMaxCount + mailboxMaxSize are PER MAILBOX (per recipient) — over cap, drop oldest and log. mailboxMaxSize is a size string: plain bytes or KB/MB/GB, space optional, decimals OK ('16MB', '12.5 MB', '1 GB', '1048576'). persistSubscriptions defaults ON (durable subscriptions, so a returning session keeps them and register_self hands its topics back); set false to opt out. devReadableKeys uses human-readable mailbox/claim folder names (slug + 4-char hash) instead of sha256 — DEV ONLY, default false; keys are format-prefixed (r-/h-) and lookups check both forms, so flipping it never strands existing mailboxes.
